title: hakea teretifolia subsp. teretifolia
intro: Hakea teretifolia subsp. teretifolia, also known as the prickly hakea, is a shrub or small tree native to Western Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and flowers of the prickly hakea are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Hakea teretifolia subsp. teretifolia can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are sometimes used in traditional medicine, although further research is necessary to fully understand its potential applications.
